content: Self-Refine’s iterative output polishing and Reflexion’s dynamic memory self-reflection can be combined to form a recursive self-improvement cycle: after Self-Refine polishes the LLM’s task output, Reflexion stores the structured lessons from successful and failed refinement attempts in its dynamic memory, which in turn guides future Self-Refine iterations to prioritize more impactful revisions rather than re-testing ineffective changes. This creates a self-teaching loop that requires no external supervision or new training, building on the core strengths of both frameworks.
